overallSummary: Fabio Giglietto maintains a robust multi-platform social media presence focused primarily on academic research in computational social science, disinformation, and information operations. With a combined follower count of 3,580 and 7 recent posts, the profile shows consistent activity. Engagement is strongest on Bluesky, indicating a highly receptive audience there, while LinkedIn provides the broadest reach for professional announcements. Content generally revolves around research updates, funding news, and career opportunities.

contentThemes.description: Posts related to new tools (CooRnet), methodological discussions, and computational approaches for social media analysis.
Content discussing studies on disinformation, coordinated link sharing, coordinated inauthentic behavior, and information operations.
Announcements about new grants, paper publications, conference presentations, and hiring calls for postdoctoral researchers or PhD students.

recommendations: Cross-promote key academic achievements (e.g., new papers, grants) across all platforms, tailoring the announcement style to each platform's audience and incorporating relevant hashtags.

Increase engagement on Mastodon by actively participating in relevant academic discussions and federated communities, perhaps by posing questions or sharing insights from ongoing research beyond just announcements.

Leverage LinkedIn's larger audience for high-impact professional news like grants and hiring, while considering sharing more behind-the-scenes research insights or thought leadership pieces to further engage the professional network.

Continue to utilize Bluesky's strength in research dissemination and opportunities, possibly by engaging in more threads or Q&A sessions related to published work or hiring calls to maximize visibility and interaction.
Consider creating short video summaries or visual abstracts for key publications and grants, as visual content often performs well across platforms and can boost engagement.

platformInsights.bluesky: Bluesky exhibits the highest average engagement rate (6.14%), indicating a highly active and receptive audience. Posts announcing new papers and research opportunities perform exceptionally well, suggesting this platform is effective for direct research dissemination and recruitment.
platformInsights.mastodon: Mastodon has a solid, albeit smaller, engaged academic community (average engagement rate 4.55%). Content related to specific tools (like CooRnet) and methodological discussions resonate strongly, suggesting a niche audience interested in technical details and academic discourse.
platformInsights.linkedin: LinkedIn has the largest follower count (1,850) and performs very well for formal professional announcements like grant awards and hiring calls (average engagement rate 4.87%). It serves as the primary platform for broad professional reach and career-oriented updates.

topPerformingContent.content: Just published our new paper on coordinated inauthentic behavior on social media platforms!
New research opportunity for PhD students interested in computational social science. Reply for details!
Excited to announce that our research team has been awarded a new grant to study information operations across social media platforms.
Our team has just released v2.0 of CooRnet, with improved algorithms for detecting coordinated link sharing behavior.
